items.title: Tasmania's net zero could be a 'temporary accounting outcome', review finds

children.content: Tasmania loves to boast about its net zero emissions. But a new report has found that status could be "temporary" if further actions are not taken.

avatar.alt: A woman with dark hair smiles.

items.title: Tasmania passes emergency law that allows fuel price cap

children.content: A bill to give the Tasmanian government power to cap fuel prices has passed the state's parliament after a late-night sitting.

image.altText: A petrol station

items.title: Federal government endorses Macquarie Point key precinct plans

children.content: Federal funding for Hobart's waterfront redevelopment and stadium has reached a key milestone, with the Australian government accepting the site's master and housing plans.

image.altText: Macquarie Point render, Stadium Aerial

items.title: TassieCast: The manosphere creeping into Tassie classrooms

children.content: The manosphere is reaching into Tassie classrooms, fuelling misogyny and anti-social behaviour.
How and why are men, particular young men, getting caught up in it web?

gallery.altText: Graphic of boy on phone while men yell in background

thumbnail.altText: Graphic of boy on phone while men yell in background

items.title: Lift minimum wage above the rate of inflation, federal government urges

children.content: In its submission to the Fair Work Commission's annual wage review, the government again calls for the lowest-paid workers to not go backwards.

image.altText: A woman speaks to the media.

items.title: WA coast 'pummelled' as Cyclone Narelle could reach category five on approach

children.content: Authorities in Western Australia say they are preparing for weather not seen since 2021, when a deadly cyclone flattened buildings in a town unaccustomed to such extreme conditions.

articles.title: Track Cyclone Narelle live: Where is it forecast to cross WA coast?

children.content: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it heads towards the WA coast.

image.altText: An illustration shows a cyclone icon above the Kimberley coast in the north of Western Australia

articles.title: Seasons clash as polar blast joins summer cyclone on Australia's weather map

children.content: A clash of seasons is developing on Australia's weather map this week as a tropical cyclone tracks towards the WA west coast and a polar blast with thunderstorms forms over the south-east.

image.altText: A graphic of Australia with a cyclone icon on WA's west coast and a blue mass above Tasmania and far south-east.

items.title: Fuel costs drive COVID-like surge in demand for emergency relief

children.content: Rising petrol and diesel prices are driving Canberra families to seek emergency assistance and forcing small businesses to consider whether to pass on higher costs to customers.

image.altText: A Vinnie's worker from behind.

items.title: Gold Coast mayor yet to decide if he'll vote on Trump tower after gifts

children.content: Tom Tate has been pressed on whether he would exclude himself from chambers if the Trump tower came to a vote, after he received gifts from the organisation.

image.altText: Man in suit smiles joyfully

items.title: Drug-fuelled driver who hit schoolboys sues Commonwealth over arrest

children.content: Tayler Hazell, who will be sentenced tomorrow over a hit-and-run that seriously injured two children, is suing the federal government, alleging police violently treated him during his arrest.

image.altText: Bodycam arret Tayler Hazell

items.title: Man who allegedly lured actors into sex acts handed 45 more charges

children.content: Police have laid 45 more charges dating back to 2014 against a Sydney man accused of posing as a Disney casting agent and offering movie opportunities for sex acts, with a magistrate describing it as "some of the most manipulative behaviour I've seen".

image.altText: a man is taken outof a police van after being arrested forposing as an agent to allegely lure people for sex

items.title: The One Nation vote in SA

children.content: One Nation just sent a shiver through the Liberal Party, after a breakthrough result in South Australia.
Pauline Hanson's party achieved more than 21 per cent of the statewide primary vote, beating the Liberal Party and becoming the second-highest polling party.
But nationally, more attention has been on the undercard
fight, and that has left the Liberal Party deeply wounded.

image.altText: TN_OneNation_Loop

items.title: Vegetable growers won't plant more crops if costs keep rising

children.content: A snap survey by AUSVEG reveals 27 per cent of Australian vegetable growers have already reduced planting, while some will not harvest current crops because rising costs make it unviable.

image.altText: Vegetable farmer Steve Kluck holds two lettuces in his hands.

items.title: TGA proposes removing sunscreen SPF numbers in regulation changes

children.content: The proposals include a number of changes to SPF testing requirements, oversight of the labs that do SPF testing, and the way SPF is labelled — reforms that would overhaul the sunscreen industry.

image.altText: bottle of sunscreen

items.title: Here's who you should see at comedy festivals across Australia

children.content: Comedy season is in full swing, with festivals across the country hosting the best acts from Australia and the world. We asked our favourites to share their recommendations.

items.title: What should you eat to keep your brain sharp as you get older?

children.content: The food choices we make over decades — not just in later life, but across adulthood — may quietly shape the health of our brains in ways that only become visible much later.

image.altText: A bowl of salad - cherry tomatoes, lettuce, sweet potato, capsicum and cabbage

items.title: AFL concussion class action expands to more than 100 former players

children.content: Eight more footballers have joined a multimillion-dollar concussion class action against the AFL, tipping the total to over 100 former players who all claim they have been left brain-damaged by the game.

articles.title: AFL says new illicit drug policy is 'most stringent in Australian sport'

children.content: The AFL says its new illicit drugs policy, which includes biannual hair-testing of all players, is the most stringent in Australian sport.

image.altText: Darcy Moore and Andrew Dillon at a press conference

articles.title: Ryswyk says reaction to coming out has been 'overwhelming'

children.content: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk says the support he received after becoming the first AFL player to come out as gay has "blown me away".

image.altText: Leigh Ryswyk, North Adelaide Football Club, about to kick a ball in an Aussie rules match

gallery.altText: Max Rooke kicks during an Australian Rules football match.

items.title: Video captures dramatic moment crane collapses in powerful storm

children.content: The crane was buffeted by a powerful storm before the boom came loose and fell onto a nearby apartment building.

image.altText: A crane bent over near a building, with a grey sky in the background.

items.title: 'My left foot was in his mouth': Fisher's lucky escape from 3.5m shark

children.content: A fisher known as "Lucky Luke" to his mates lives up to his nickname after surviving a great white shark dragging him along the ocean floor while lobster fishing off the South Australian coast.

image.altText: A man a black and red t-shirt holds two rock lobsters in front of a boat

items.title: Fire-ravaged shire demands fossil fuel companies foot climate disaster bill

children.content: A Victorian council still cleaning up after a bushfire that wiped out more than 50 homes and businesses wants fossil fuel companies to pay for climate disaster recovery.

image.altText: Lucas standing in front of a garden wearing a green jacket.

items.title: History-making jockey Michelle Payne honoured with statue at Flemington

children.content: Australian horse racing trailblazer Michelle Payne, the first woman to win the Melbourne Cup, has been honoured with a statue unveiled today.

image.altText: Former Melbourne Cup winner Michelle Payne poses next to a statue dedicated to her

items.title: Discerning crocs from logs the challenge for AI trial

children.content: Researchers are trialling artificial intelligence to detect crocodiles in north Queensland waterways, but its success depends on human training to ensure accuracy.

gallery.altText: A crocodile slips into the water in the Kakadu National Park.

thumbnail.altText: A crocodile slips into the water in the Kakadu National Park.

items.title: Popovic wants World Cup contenders to play with freedom in final home matches

children.content: The Socceroos' upcoming games against Cameroon and Curaçao could make or shatter World Cup dreams, and Socceroos legend Mark Schwarzer has backed the players, and coach Tony Popovic, to deliver come tournament time.

image.altText: Josip Skoko, Jacob Italiano, Patrick Yazbek, Mark Schwarzer hold an 'Australia' flag and pose in front of Sydney Opera House

items.title: The NRL risks losing itself in a maze of set restarts

children.content: After three rounds of the NRL season the increase in set restarts has left coaches, players and fans alike more confused than ever about the specifics behind rugby league's most controversial rule change.

image.altText: An NRL referee waves for a set restart during a match

items.title: Review calls for overhaul of WA's 'strictest' guardianship secrecy laws

children.content: People living under a guardianship arrangement in WA can't publicly speak up, but a review says this has to change.

gallery.altText: A collage of three photos of people with their faces scratched out.

thumbnail.altText: A collage of three photos of people with their faces scratched out.

items.title: Bay the 'greatest risk and biggest asset' for beachside residents in Victoria

children.content: For residents of Victoria's Bellarine Peninsula like Vicki, who is facing the impacts of climate change-induced erosion and inundation, the water near their doors is both their biggest asset and greatest risk.

image.altText: Vicki Perrett stands in her driveway with the ocean in the background.

items.title: Hard time for renters as landlords squeeze tight market

children.content: Australian renters continue to have a hard time finding a home, despite vacancy rates slowly improving from post COVID pandemic lows, a report finds.

image.altText: A For Lease sign advertising a rental property in Carlton North.

items.title: 'Here I am': Sarah Mullally enthroned as first female Archbishop of Canterbury

children.content: Dame Sarah Mullally is enthroned as the Church of England's first female Archbishop of Canterbury, taking the helm as the spiritual leader of 85 million Anglicans worldwide in a historic ceremony blending tradition with global symbolism.

articles.title: Australian bishop surprised by appointment to senior Vatican role

children.content: Anthony Randazzo, the bishop of Broken Bay, which since 2019 covers Sydney's northern regions and the central coast, receives the personal title of archbishop and is named as Prefect of the Dicastery for Legislative Texts.
